evals.prompt: Break this idea into Jira stories: customers can save items for later in checkout, see that list across devices, and move items back into the cart when they are ready to buy.
evals.expected_output: A vertical-slice story set that starts from customer outcomes, sequences thin end-to-end slices, avoids frontend/backend/database task stories, and produces Jira-ready stories with acceptance criteria, out-of-scope boundaries, and suggested sizes.

evals.prompt: I need stories for adding manager approval to refunds. My initial breakdown is: build refund approval API, add DB table, add UI modal, add audit logging. Rewrite this into proper Jira stories.
evals.expected_output: A rewritten backlog that rejects the horizontal technical-task framing, groups work into customer- or operator-visible vertical stories, identifies any true enabling chores/spikes separately, and explains the slicing rationale.

evals.prompt: Turn this design into stories: store associates need to look up a customer's online order in-store, view fulfillment status, and resend a pickup barcode if the customer can't find it. We are not sure whether the current order service exposes barcode regeneration yet.
evals.expected_output: A story set that separates clear value slices from uncertainty, creates Jira-ready customer-facing stories first, and identifies a spike only for the unresolved barcode-regeneration capability.
